soal: The more you take, the more you leave behind. What is it?
Has a neck but no head. What is it?
Can travel around the world while staying in a corner. What is it?
Has teeth but cannot bite. What is it?
Always in front of you but cannot be seen. What is it?
Can be broken without being touched. What is it?
Has hands but cannot clap. What is it?
Gets wetter the more it dries. What is it?
Has an eye but cannot see. What is it?
Has keys but no locks, space but no room. What is it?
I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. What am I?
The more you feed it the bigger it grows; give it water and it dies. What is it?
Has cities but no houses, mountains but no trees, water but no fish. What is it?
Runs but has no legs. What is it?
What has one head, one foot, and four legs?
The person who makes it does not need it. The person who buys it does not use it. The person who uses it does not know it. What is it?
If you drop me I will crack, but smile at me and I will smile back. What am I?
Full of holes but still holds water. What is it?
The harder you hit it the bigger it gets. What is it?
Has a thumb and four fingers but is not alive. What is it?
Light as a feather, yet even the strongest person cannot hold it for more than a few minutes. What is it?
Goes up but never comes down. What is it?
Has a head and a tail but no body. What is it?
What is so fragile that even saying its name breaks it?
Has words but never speaks. What is it?
Three lives have I. Gentle enough to soothe the skin, light enough to caress the sky, hard enough to crack rocks. What am I?
The more you have of it, the less you see. What is it?
Always old, sometimes new, never sad, sometimes blue. Never empty, sometimes full, never pushing, always pulling. What am I?
What can run but never walks, has a mouth but never talks, has a head but never weeps, has a bed but never sleeps?
What belongs to you but other people use it more than you do?
